revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛭児神社 (霧島市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Hiruko Jinja Kirishima.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Hayato|qid=Q5686146|1=Hayato, Kagoshima}}|coordinates={{Coord|31.761222|N|130.750167|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}
'''Hiruko Shrine''' (蛭児神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Hayato, Kagoshima|en|Hayato, Kagoshima|qid=Q5686146|lt=Hayato Town|1=Hayato, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kirishima, Kagoshima|en|Kirishima, Kagoshima|qid=Q858352|lt=Kirishima City|1=Kirishima, Kagoshima}}, {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture|en|Kagoshima Prefecture|qid=Q15701|lt=Kagoshima Prefecture|1=Kagoshima Prefecture}}. Until the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was considered the {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|qid=Q841389|lt=Second Shrine (Ninomiya)|1=Ninomiya}} of {{ill|Ōsumi Province|en|Ōsumi Province|qid=Q981002|lt=Ōsumi Province|1=Ōsumi Province}}, following {{ill|Kagoshima Shrine|en|Kagoshima Shrine|qid=Q704695|lt=Kagoshima Shrine|1=Kagoshima Jingū}}, and was known as the "Ninomiya Daimyōjin" (二之宮大明神).<ref name="San32">Kenkō Hashiguchi, Hidetaka Godai, Kanetsugu Hashiguchi, ''{{ill|Sangoku Meishō Zue|ja|三国名勝図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sangoku Meishō Zue|1=Sangoku meisho zue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11354726}}'', Volume 32, 1843.</ref>
== Deity Enshrined == The shrine is dedicated to {{ill|Hiruko-no-Mikoto|ja|蛭児尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578995|1=Hiruko}}.
== History == The shrine is said to have been founded during the {{ill|Age of the Gods|ja|神代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Age of the Gods|qq=}}, though the current shrine grounds were reportedly established during the {{ill|Sengū|en|Sengū|qid=Q2271023|lt=Sengū|1=Sengū}} reconstruction in {{ill|Kan'en 3 (1750)|ja|寛延3年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'en 3 (1750)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578998|1=1750}}.<ref>On-site information board.</ref> According to legend, the deity, a divine child born to {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}}, drifted here aboard the heavenly rock camphor ship (天磐橡樟船, ''Amano Iwakusu no Fune''). It is said that the ship sprouted branches and leaves, growing into a giant tree. The current sacred {{ill|camphor tree|ja|楠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/camphor tree|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クスノキ|1=Camphora officinarum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q158722}} was replanted in {{ill|Kyōhō 13 (1728)|ja|享保13年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōhō 13 (1728)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579000|1=1728}} by Kabayama Kazue Hisahatsu, the local magistrate of {{ill|Kokubu|ja|国分|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokubu|1=Kokubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420493}} (later Kokubu City).
The shrine grounds also feature a golden-striped bamboo known as "Kinsujiku," believed to have grown from the fishing rod or pole the deity used while traveling.
== Surroundings == * Several {{ill|Sea-creature grape mirrors|ja|海獣葡萄鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sea-creature grape mirrors|1=Sea-beast grape mirror|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11151137}} (bronze mirrors) believed to date back to the {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} were excavated from nearby ruins. * Adjacent to the shrine is the reconstructed inn where {{ill|Saigō Takamori|en|Saigō Takamori|qid=Q310462|lt=Saigō Takamori|1=Saigō Takamori}} once stayed, known as "Saigō-don no Yado" (西郷どんの宿). * The nearby {{ill|Hinatayama Onsen|ja|日当山温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinatayama Onsen|1=Hinatayama Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505316}} is said to be a place where the enshrined deity bathed for healing purposes. * The surrounding area was once known as "Nameke no Mori" (奈毛気の杜) or "Nameki no Kamimura" (奈毛木神叢), celebrated for its scenic beauty in Ōsumi Province.<ref name="San32"/>
== References == <references/>
== Further Reading == * Tomosaburō Mitsuishi, ''Hayato Kyōdoshi'' (隼人郷土誌), pp. 12–13, Hayato Town Office, 1985.
== External Links == * [http://www.kagojinjacho.or.jp/search/airaisa/kirishima/post-869.html Hiruko Shrine] - {{ill|Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office|ja|鹿児島県神社庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima Prefecture Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社庁|1=Jinjachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11590703}} (in Japanese) * {{commonscat|Hiruko-jinja (Kirishima)}}
{{Shinto}} {{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|蛭児神社 (霧島市)|version=91104012|comment=Imported full ja history}}
